commits.body:
User asked "did you check the backend repo's code?" — earlier audit
was shallow.  Did a proper read of tool-till-tees@f7b2f29 via the
GitHub API:

- api/sancio/[...path].ts (full catch-all dispatcher)
- lib/routes/sancio/{projects,agreements,notifications}.ts
- lib/cors.ts, lib/auth.ts
- tsconfig.json, package.json, vercel.json
- recent commit log (last 15 commits, 2026-04-22 to 2026-04-23)

Findings:

- Code on `main` is correct.  Catch-all dispatches segments[0]
  matches to the right handlers.  Named exports match imports.
  ESM resolution is fine.  No recent commit touched the dispatch.
- The string "Not found" (no prefix) emits in exactly one place:
  the catch-all final fallthrough.  Every resource handler uses
  prefixed messages ("Project not found", etc.).  The report's
  log line therefore points unambiguously at the fallthrough.
- Coverage gap: api/__tests__/sancio/*.test.ts imports handler
  functions directly and bypasses the dispatcher entirely.  A bug
  in the segment-matching cascade in [...path].ts would NOT be
  caught by tool-till-tees' npm test.

Updates to docs:

- USER_ACTIONS.md — root cause ranking expanded from 4 to 5 with
  the audit's findings; added a one-line defensive fix proposal
  for tool-till-tees that handles req.query.path being a string,
  array, or undefined regardless of root cause.
- SESSION_NOTES.md — captured what the backend audit covered + its
  conclusion (deployment mismatch, not code bug) so the next
  session doesn't re-walk the same ground.

commits.subject: docs(session): correct false firewall diagnosis, document 404 handoff
commits.body:
Earlier in this session I documented the API 404s as a Vercel platform
firewall on tool-till-tees, citing "403 x-deny-reason: host_not_allowed"
from curl probes.  That diagnosis was wrong: the 403s came from the
Claude Code sandbox's own network proxy, not from Vercel.  Every
external host returns the same response from inside the sandbox,
including google.com.

Corrected USER_ACTIONS.md entry:

- The user's actual symptom is 404 with body {"error": "Not found"}.
  That string only appears in tool-till-tees/api/sancio/[...path].ts's
  final fallthrough, so the request reached the function but no segment
  branch matched.
- Lists the four most-likely root causes (stale tool-till-tees deploy,
  rewrite mistranscription losing the path segment, trailing-slash
  redirect, deploy alias mismatch) and concrete diagnostic curl + browser
  steps that must be run from outside the sandbox.

SESSION_NOTES.md rewritten as a handoff for the next session:

- Flags the sandbox-network constraint up front so the next agent
  doesn't repeat the same diagnostic mistake.
- Pins the 404's source string to its single emit site for fast triage.
- Lists what "fix all" did and didn't cover (5 iOS-zoom fixes shipped;
  the 404 is unfixable from this repo alone; no specific viewport bug
  was described — ask before guessing).
- Documents three latent mobile concerns (iOS keyboard over fixed save
  bar, NotificationBell 100vw, DaisyUI modal-box vh-not-dvh) that
  weren't triggered by the current report but are worth eyes-on if more
  reports land.

commits.subject: fix(ui): suppress iOS focus-zoom on five sub-16px form controls
commits.body:
iOS Safari auto-zooms into any <input> / <textarea> with computed
font-size < 16px on focus. Input.tsx / TextArea.tsx wrappers already
enforce text-base, but five bare elements bypassed that guard:

- ContractUploadPreview.tsx:294  text-sm -> text-base   (rename input)
- AgreementHeader.tsx:66         text-sm -> text-base   (description)
- ProjectDetailPage.tsx:92       text-sm -> text-base   (description)
- ModuleSummaryEditor.tsx:120    +text-base on input-sm (DaisyUI v5
                                  input-sm is 14px; min-h-[44px]
                                  fixes height but not font-size)
- ToneSelector.tsx:128           +text-base on input-sm (same)

All five keep their compact visual variants (input-sm or text-sm
removed in favor of text-base on full-size); only rendered font-size
changes from 14px to 16px so iOS no longer zooms.  min-h-[44px]
already in place on each so the touch-target rule (CLAUDE.md
"User Experience (Non-Negotiable)") is unaffected.

Audit triggered by mobile debug report at 360x674 / Android Chrome 147.
Same report also surfaced GET /api/sancio/* -> 404; that's a
tool-till-tees Vercel platform firewall (now returning 403
x-deny-reason: host_not_allowed) and is recorded in
docs/USER_ACTIONS.md as a backend dashboard fix.  Frontend rewrites
+ client are correct (apiRewrites.test.ts green).

commits.body:
First-visit users with no stored preference now land on dark mode
regardless of OS prefers-color-scheme. Mirror image of the previous

- index.html bootstrap: storedDark === 'true' → storedDark !== 'false'.

  background_color: #0082ce / #ffffff → #202020 / #202020 (business

  who never toggle.

  in index.html; failure overlay in main.tsx) flipped to the dark
  palette so the fallback path remains coherent if the bootstrap script
  is blocked (CSP, JS disabled).
- src/index.css plugin comment + CLAUDE.md THEME_DARK_MODE section
  rewritten to document the dark-first policy and the recovery paths
  (light-first revert + OS-sync restore).

Existing users: anyone who explicitly toggled (storage has darkMode=
true|false) keeps their preference. Anyone who never toggled (no
storage key) flips to dark on next visit.

Follow-up to 2ab4e0b. Previous commit flipped the runtime theme default
from OS-driven to light-first, but left the PWA manifest's install-time
`theme_color` at `#202020` (business base-100, dark). That created a

followed by light app, which is the exact jarring flash the light-first

  status bar + white background; app opens in light mode with the
  same blue status bar. Consistent.

  light splash → dark app status bar transition. Trade-off accepted
  per the light-first policy.

instruction if the light-first policy ever reverts.

commits.subject: feat(theme): light-first default on first visit — ignore OS prefers-color-scheme
commits.body:
New policy: first-visit users always land on light mode regardless of
their OS theme preference. Dark mode requires an explicit toggle via
the burger menu, which is then persisted and wins on every subsequent
visit across all devices sharing the browser profile. Rationale: users
wanted a predictable landing experience — OS-driven theme flips were
jarring for people bouncing between devices with different OS settings,
especially since Sancio content (agreement titles, counterparty names)
doesn't adapt cleanly to being viewed at wildly different color
temperatures between sessions.

Four touchpoints in the theme resolution chain, all updated to agree:

1. src/index.css — DaisyUI plugin registration. Dropped `--prefersdark`
   from the `business` theme so DaisyUI's own first-paint fallback no
   longer swaps to dark on OS-dark systems when the JS bootstrap is
   blocked. `corporate --default` alone wins.

2. index.html — inline bootstrap script. Simplified the dark-state
   resolution from "stored ?? OS media query" to just `storedDark ===
   "true"`. Unset localStorage means light, always. Also collapsed the
   two `<meta name="theme-color">` tags (media-queried by OS prefer-
   ence) into a single non-queried `content="#0082ce"` tag so the
   pre-JS status bar is also light-first.

3. src/utils/theme.ts:
   - Removed `prefersDarkMedia()` helper entirely (no longer called).
   - `resolveInitial()` now returns `{ dark: false, source: 'default' }`
     when no stored preference exists (was `{ dark: prefersDarkMedia(),
     source: 'os' }`).
   - `setCombo()` and `toggleDark()` coalesce `null` storage to `false`
     (was `prefersDarkMedia()`).
   - `initTheme()` no longer attaches a `matchMedia('(prefers-color-
     scheme: dark)')` change listener — OS flips don't propagate.
   - Cross-tab sync's "both keys cleared elsewhere" branch now
     applies light instead of re-resolving from OS.
   - Added file-header note capturing the rationale + a clear comment
     at the removed-listener site explaining how to restore OS-sync
     if the policy ever reverses.

4. CLAUDE.md "Adopted Patterns → THEME_DARK_MODE" — added a
   "Light-first default (deliberate)" paragraph naming the four files
   that together enforce the policy. A future audit or contributor
   considering adding OS-sync back has a checklist to follow.

User-experience summary:
- First visit on any OS: light mode.
- User toggles dark via burger menu: `darkMode: "true"` persists;
  every future visit renders dark.
- User toggles back: `darkMode: "false"` persists.
- User clears site data: back to light-first on next visit.
- Cross-tab: toggling in one tab propagates to others via `storage`
  event; clearing keys in one tab reverts siblings to light-first.

Tests 155/155, tsc -b clean, eslint clean, `npm run build` clean.

commits.subject: i18n: fix pluralization consistency + swap directional classes to logical
commits.body:
Two of three audit findings applied. formatRelativeTime rewrite
skipped — pushed back because Intl.RelativeTimeFormat changes visible
text ("1 min ago" → "1 min. ago" in short style, or "1 minute ago" in
long) and none of the styles match the current UX exactly. The audit
itself said "leave unless you want it"; I followed that judgement.

MEDIUM (1) — pluralization inconsistency / latent zero-count bug:

Six sites in src/ use `!== 1 ? 's' : ''` (correct English — handles
0, 1, 2+ properly). Two sites in AgreementDetailPage (line 228 +
line 249) used `> 1 ? 's' : ''` which would render "0 module"
instead of "0 modules" if ever called with an empty count. In
practice both upstream paths (SplitAgreementForm refuses 0
selections at line 36, batchToggle only fires from non-empty
multi-select UI) validate 1+, so the bug couldn't fire. Still fixed
for consistency — same pattern everywhere, defence against future
validation changes.

LOW (1) — directional → logical classes:

11 uses of `ml-*` / `mr-*` / `pl-*` / `pr-*` / `left-*` / `right-*`
across 10 files, all logical spacing/positioning (not icon-
directionality). Swapped to Tailwind logical-property variants:

- ml-* → ms- (margin-inline-start): NudgeExpiryConfig helper text
  indent, SigningStatus timestamp, SequenceSignOff timestamp,
  SplitAgreementForm "Signed" pill, ContractUploadPreview oversized
  warning
- mr-* → me- (margin-inline-end): GoogleLoginButton icon-before-text
- pl-* → ps- (padding-inline-start): ContractUploadPreview action
  button indentation
- pr-* → pe- (padding-inline-end): ContractUploadPreview scroll
  container
- right-0 → end-0 (inset-inline-end): BurgerMenu dropdown anchor,
  NotificationBell dropdown anchor (both should anchor to the
  screen edge of the trigger in either LTR or RTL)
- left-0 right-0 → inset-x-0: ModuleEditor sticky save bar (full-
  width horizontal span)

Identical rendering in LTR; automatically mirrors under dir="rtl" if
the app ever adopts an RTL locale. Final grep confirms zero
directional classes remain.

Hardcoded English strings + RTL layout beyond what logical-class

commits.subject: fix(proposals): counterparty-accept now replaces module content (parity with creator-accept)
commits.body:
Bug surfaced by the `copy` trigger audit. Left unfixed in the copy
commit because the symptom was a toast inconsistency but the root
cause was a missing state mutation — a behaviour bug, not a copy
issue.

The proposal flow:

1. Counterparty creates Proposal A (proposed_by: counterparty.id,
   proposed_content: their change request).
2. Creator reviews. Three resolutions available:
   - Accept → updateModule({ content: A.proposed_content }) +
     resolveProposal(A, 'accepted'). Module becomes A's body. ✓
   - Reject → resolveProposal(A, 'rejected'). No content change.
   - Counter → resolveProposal(A, 'countered', <creator's text>) AND
     createProposal(…) as Proposal B (proposed_by: creator.id,
     proposed_content: creator's counter text).
3. Counterparty reviews Proposal B. Can accept / reject / counter.

The creator's accept-path (ModuleEditorPage:169-172) correctly does
updateModule + resolveProposal before the toast. The counterparty's
accept-path (lines 312-315 pre-fix) ONLY resolved the proposal —
never called updateModule. Accepting the creator's counter was a
no-op on module content.

ProposalCard gates the Accept button with `status === 'pending' &&
!isOwnProposal`, so this path only fires when the counterparty is
accepting a proposal they didn't author. In practice that means
they're accepting a creator-authored counter, and
`proposal.proposed_content` is the target body — same semantics as
the creator branch accepting a counterparty-authored original.

Fix: mirror the creator branch — updateModule +
resolveProposal + matching toast. Added a brief in-code comment
explaining the symmetry so a future reviewer sees why both branches
write updateModule before resolveProposal.

Removed the corresponding `## Behaviour Asymmetry` entry from
docs/TODO.md since it's fixed.

No new test: ModuleEditorPage doesn't have a component test yet (both
creator and counterparty branches share ProposalCard + useProposals +
useModules, which would all need mocking for an end-to-end accept
assertion). The fix is one line mirroring an existing working
pattern; verified against the creator branch's contract in the
audit above.

Tests 155/155 passing, tsc -b clean, eslint clean.

- ProjectDetailPage: "Failed to save changes" → "...— try again."
- AgreementHeader: "Failed to save changes" → "...— try again."
- AgreementDetailPage: 3 sites — update agreement status, generate
  verification code, split agreement — all get "— try again."
- TemplateSelector parse-error fallback: the fallback only fires when
  parseDocument surfaced no message of its own (the library-supplied
  message is already user-readable). Extended copy: "Failed to read
  this file. Try a different file, or convert it to .txt / .docx /

  submitting" → "No changes yet — edit the content to submit your
  proposal." Reads like a peer prompt rather than a form-validator
  string; names the same constraint more directly.

NOT a copy fix — a behaviour asymmetry surfaced by the audit:

The audit flagged "Proposal accepted" vs "Proposal accepted —
module content updated" as inconsistent. On inspection, the two
toasts accurately describe two DIFFERENT behaviours:

- ModuleEditorPage:169-172 (creator accepts a counterparty proposal)
  calls updateModule(mod.id, { content: proposal.proposed_content })
  THEN resolveProposal(...). Module content updates. Toast says so.
- ModuleEditorPage:312-314 (counterparty accepts a creator's
  counter-proposal) calls resolveProposal(...) only. No updateModule.
  Toast correctly says just "Proposal accepted".

The toasts are right. The missing updateModule call on the
counterparty branch is almost certainly a bug — accepting a
counter-proposal should replace module content with the counter's
body, same as the creator path. Logged to docs/TODO.md under a new
"Behaviour Asymmetry" section so it gets investigated as a bug
(needs verification of intent + either a fix or explanatory
comment), not patched as a copy issue.

  payload / session / JWT / token / endpoint / CRUD / RLS — no
  matches across src/).
- Voice consistent: sentence-case, active verbs, no marketing copy.
- All handshake / empty / signing state copy was already solid.

commits.subject: forms: apply all 8 audit findings — error handling, a11y labels, autofill hints
commits.body:
Forms-trigger pass. Every touched form now has proper async error surfacing, screen-reader-announced live counters, and accessibility-hinted autofill behaviour. HIGH (3) - ProposeChangeForm: onSubmit prop widened to `void | Promise`; handleSubmit awaits it inside try/catch/finally. On failure: error surfaces above the submit button ("Failed to submit — try again in a moment"). `submitting` state gates the button + flips label to "Submitting…" so double-clicks can't fire duplicate proposals. - AddNoteForm: same pattern. Label flips to "Adding…" during flight. - NudgeExpiryConfig: added `inputMode="numeric"` so iOS/Android show the numeric keypad instead of QWERTY for the 1-365 nudge-days field. MEDIUM (3) - Character counters on ProposeChangeForm, AddNoteForm, ModuleEditor, ModuleSummaryEditor now carry `role="status" aria-atomic="true"`. `role="status"` implies polite aria-live; `aria-atomic="true"` forces the full "N / MAX" text to be announced on every update rather than just the changed characters. Screen readers throttle polite announcements so keystroke-level updates don't become chatter — but users near the cap get the feedback they need. - SplitAgreementForm: the checkbox group is now a proper
with , and the error

gets `id="split-agreement-error"` + `role="alert"`. The fieldset adds `aria-describedby` pointing at the error id when an error is present. Assistive tech now associates "Please select at least one module" with the group that failed. - ContractUploadPreview rename input: explicit `aria-label={`Rename section (currently "${mod.title}")`}` on the input itself. Previously users who reached the input via direct keyboard focus (not through the preceding rename button) lost the context the button's aria-label had provided. LOW (2) - InviteCounterpartyForm email field: `autoComplete="email"`, `inputMode="email"`, `autoCapitalize="none"`, `autoCorrect="off"`, `spellCheck={false}`. Mobile autofill + browser-level email recognition now work; typing stops trying to sentence-case the first letter. Passphrase field: `autoComplete="off"` (this is a side-channel shared secret, not a real password — we don't want password managers saving it), plus the same no-capitalize/no-correct/ no-spellcheck companions. Comment explains the intent so a future reviewer doesn't add `autoComplete="current-password"` by default. - ModuleEditor empty-content validation: left unvalidated per the audit's intent question. Added a comment in handleSave explaining why content emptiness is acceptable (skeleton modules / reverted drafts) and naming the guards that do exist (title-required + disabled-unless-hasChanges). Tests 155/155 passing — vi.fn() in ProposeChangeForm + AddNoteForm tests awaits as undefined, so the async widening is backward- compatible with existing mocks. tsc -b clean, eslint clean. commits.subject: a11y(motion): boot-loader spinner respects prefers-reduced-motion
commits.body:
index.html hosts the pre-React loading spinner in an inline <style>
block that renders before Vite injects src/index.css. The app's
reduced-motion rules live in index.css, so the spinner kept spinning
through the boot window (~0.2-2s, longer on slow connections) even
when the user had reduced-motion enabled at the OS level.

Fix:
- Added `boot-spinner` class to the animating <div>.
- Extended the inline <style> block with a scoped reduced-motion rule:
  `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){ .boot-spinner{animation:
  none !important} }`.
- Left an explanatory comment so a future edit doesn't wonder why the
  keyframes live in the HTML rather than the CSS bundle.

Post-mount, index.css's global reduced-motion rules take over for all
DaisyUI + custom animations (already verified compliant in the motion
audit).

No test lock on the boot-loader markup; iconCacheBustHtml's literal
replacements target <link rel="icon|apple-touch-icon"> tags, not the
<style> block, so the plugin ran cleanly through build.

Tests 155/155, tsc clean, eslint clean, npm run build clean.

commits.body:
All findings from the mobile trigger's WCAG 2.5.5 audit — every one is
a compact DaisyUI variant (`btn-sm` / `input-sm`) that was rendering
below 44px touch-target without the matching `min-h-[44px]` override.

Size fixes (6):

- NudgeExpiryConfig nudge-days number input (HIGH) — `w-24` config
  input, primary interaction surface on the per-module nudge config.
- ModuleSummaryEditor text inputs (HIGH) — three summary fields
  ("what it does" / "when it matters" / "if not followed") rendered
  via the shared `SummaryField` component; adding min-h there fixes
  all three.
- ToneSelector custom-tone input (HIGH) — the `input-sm flex-1` users
  type custom tones/intents into. The field is used in every note +
  proposal composition flow.
- ToneSelector Add button (MEDIUM) — `btn btn-sm` next to the custom
  input. Now `min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px]` matching the NotificationBell
  + BurgerMenu trigger pattern.
- ContractUploadPreview section rename input (MEDIUM) — the inline
  rename that appears when you click a section title during upload
  preview. Less frequently used but critical when active.
- AppLayout breadcrumb links (MEDIUM) — 4px short at `min-h-[40px]`,
  bumped to `min-h-[44px]`. Mobile breadcrumb taps happen all the
  time during navigation.

Convention (1):

- CLAUDE.md "User Experience (Non-Negotiable)" — added two bullets
  codifying the touch-target rule + the DaisyUI compact-variant
  pairing convention. Also added a companion bullet on iOS
  auto-zoom prevention (text-base / 16px) since the audit verified
  Input/TextArea already enforce this and it's worth documenting so
  future ad-hoc inputs don't regress. Future mobile audits should
  have nothing to flag beyond new code that breaks the rule.

Deferred (1):

- `checkbox-sm` elements surrounded by flex-row text labels — the
  audit called these "acceptable via adjacency" pending real-device
  testing. Leaving until that testing disputes it; the convention
  codified today applies to checkboxes too if it ever does.

Verified untouched by the fixes: viewport meta, safe-area insets,
tap-delay, gesture handlers (none), prefers-reduced-motion, native
`<dialog>` + keyboard behaviour, PWA manifest, primary trigger
buttons — all still compliant.

min-h-[44px] class string specifically, so behaviour checks continue
unchanged. tsc -b clean, eslint clean.

commits.subject: fix(template-selector): defensively guard against splitSubSections stale-closure race
commits.body:
Per pushback: don't track a fixable bug as TODO when I'm in the file.

The race trigger flagged that handleFileSelect captures splitSubSections
in its closure across the parseDocument await. In the current UI, the
toggle checkbox only renders inside ContractUploadPreview (preview
state), so no user-visible path exposes the staleness — the bug is
theoretical, not reachable. But the cost of guarding is 4 lines + a
useEffect, and if the toggle is ever surfaced in the upload tab itself
(e.g. a power-user pre-toggle), the call site stays correct without
having to be re-thought.

Mechanism: mirror splitSubSections into a ref, sync via useEffect, read
the ref's current value after the parseDocument await instead of the
closure's captured value.

No regression test: the race isn't reachable from current UI, so an
RTL test can't drive it without exposing internals. The mechanism is
visible in code review and the in-file comment is honest about its
defensive nature.

Enhancements — closed by this commit.

Wrap-up of the earlier custom-code reduction session. Follows a systematic
`useCallback` audit: the codebase has zero `React.memo` usage, so a
`useCallback` is only load-bearing when paired with a custom hook whose
useEffect deps include it, or with a child component that lists the
function in its own useEffect deps. Everywhere else it's dead weight.

- App.tsx: three near-identical `min-h-* flex items-center justify-center`
  + spinner blocks collapsed into one local `Loading({ minHeightClass })`
  helper.
- ToneSelector lazy-init: `useState(getRecentTones)` →
  `useState(() => getRecentTones())` so it matches AppLayout's
  `useState(() => !navigator.onLine)`. Arrow form is the codebase
  convention now.
- useCallback dropped where the handler binds to native `<button>` /
  `<input>` or to a plain-array prop consumer:
    ContractUploadPreview (8), TemplateSelector (8),
    ProposeChangeForm.handleToggleTone, AddNoteForm.handleToggleTone,
    IntentSelector.handleToggle, ModuleSummaryEditor.handleBlur,
    BurgerMenu.handleItem + handleMenuKeyDown,
    DebugPill.handleCopy + handleClear,
    AppLayout.handleCheckForUpdate,
    AgreementDetailPage.closeModal + handleUploadModules.
- useCallback kept where a consumer's useEffect deps require identity
  stability (documented in-file where non-obvious):
    all src/hooks/* (data-layer pattern),
    BurgerMenu.close (useEscapeKey's effect deps include onEscape),
    page-level clearToast in 4 pages (Toast's effect deps include onDone).
- CLAUDE.md architecture block refreshed: removed stale `generateId`,
  added HeadingInput, formFieldId, icons.ts, safeStorage, debugLog,

- LoadingSpinner.tsx deleted. The wrapper was a `flex items-center

  pattern; multi-consumer icons live in constants, single-consumer icons
  stay next to their consumer.
- AppLayout menuItems: `useMemo` dropped. Zero `React.memo` in the
  codebase means the memoization couldn't protect any downstream
  component from re-rendering — it only added dep-array bookkeeping.
- ToneSelector handlers: `useCallback` dropped on `handleCustomSubmit`
  + `handleKeyDown`. Both pass to native `<input>` / `<button>`, which

commits.subject: chore(pwa): close remaining gaps — HMR guard, typed versioned, docs, tests
commits.body:
Addresses every deferred / out-of-scope item from the PWA hardening
session: HMR listener safety, regex coverage, type safety, test depth,
and documentation for both manual QA and end-users.

src/lib/pwa.ts:
- initPwaGlobalListeners now guards on window.__pwaGlobalListenersAttached,
  not a module-scoped _globalInit variable. Module-scope resets on Vite
  HMR and would re-attach visibilitychange + controllerchange listeners
  each hot reload, duplicating fires. Window-scope survives module
  re-evaluation — same shape as src/utils/debugLog.ts's listener guard.
- _resetPwaStateForTesting() exported so vitest can isolate module state
  between tests. Underscore-prefixed + "ForTesting" named to surface
  misuse in code search.

src/types/browser.d.ts:
- Declares Window.__pwaGlobalListenersAttached alongside the existing
  debugLog guard flag.

vite.config.ts:
- versioned() is now typed on a literal union (`IconPath`) derived from
  ICON_PATHS as const — a typo like versioned('typo.png') fails at
  compile time instead of silently shipping `?v=undefined`. Runtime
  throw added as belt-and-braces.
- Final-sweep ICON_LINK_RE widened to cover `mask-icon` (Safari pinned
  tabs) and `apple-touch-startup-image` (iOS splash). A future <link
  rel="mask-icon"> added without updating REPLACEMENTS now throws at
  build time instead of leaking un-versioned.

src/__tests__/iconCacheBust.test.ts:
- 6 new source-level tripwires: sha256 (not timestamp) hashing,
  REPLACEMENTS-literal-missing throw, un-versioned-sweep throw,
  regex covers every icon-bearing rel, versioned() runtime throw.

src/__tests__/pwaSingleton.test.ts:
- 14 new unit tests for the lib/pwa.ts singleton. Covers the 30s
  suppression window, hasUpdate / offlineReady transitions + auto-
  dismiss with vi.useFakeTimers, snapshot referential stability
  (critical for useSyncExternalStore), subscribe/unsubscribe +
  listener-isolation-on-throw, and checkForUpdate() 'no-sw' branch.
  Inline sessionStorage shim avoids pulling jsdom/happy-dom for a
  tiny test suite.

tsconfig.test.json:
- Added DOM + DOM.Iterable libs so transitive imports from src/lib/
  type-check under the test project. Added src/types/**/* to include
  so Window.__pwaGlobalListenersAttached augmentation resolves.

docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md:
- 8 new manual scenarios covering Chunk 9 + 10: visibility-based update
  detection, 30s suppression, Check for updates menu item, controller-
  change reload guard, manifest identity fields, offline icon render,
  hash stability across rebuilds, Install modal outdated-icon
  disclosure. Regression checklist extended with 10 new PWA items.

docs/USER_GUIDE.md:
- New "Installing Sancio as an App" section (platform notes for
  iOS / macOS / Android / Firefox + guidance on stale-icon issues).
- New "Checking for Updates" section explaining auto-detection vs
  manual Check for updates, with plain-language typed-result copy.

25 tests total. Build + lint + typecheck + tests all clean.

commits.subject: feat(pwa): content-hash icon URLs to bust browser / CDN / SW / WebAPK caches
commits.body:
Implements PWA_ICON_CACHE_BUST pattern. Icons referenced by stable
filenames survive "clear site data" and PWA reinstall because every
cache layer below the OS keys off URL. Adding `?v=<content-hash>` busts
4 of 5 cache layers; the 5th (OS icon cache) is surfaced to the user.

vite.config.ts:
- `iconVersion(relPath)` — sha256-8 of each icon under /public/.
  Warns (doesn't throw) on missing file so fresh clones can still
  `vite dev` before running generate-icons.
- `iconCacheBustHtml()` — Vite plugin rewrites exact-literal `href="/…"`
  on favicon.ico / favicon.png / apple-touch-icon.png to include the
  version query. Throws on missing literal so reformatted tags surface
  at build time instead of silently shipping un-versioned URLs.
- Wired BEFORE VitePWA() in the plugins array — locks the contract in.
- Manifest icons pass through `versioned()` so their src carries `?v=…`.
- workbox: ignoreURLParametersMatching: [/^v$/] so precache lookups
  still match the versioned URL (without it, offline breaks).

src/__tests__/iconCacheBust.test.ts (vitest):
- Source-level: iconCacheBustHtml wired before VitePWA; workbox has
  /^v$/; index.html still contains the literal hrefs we match.
- Dist-level (skipped when dist/ absent): manifest icons all versioned;
  index.html icon links all versioned, no bare un-versioned leaks;
  sw.js has cleanupOutdatedCaches() and /^v$/ ignore.

tsconfig.test.json:
- New config with node types, excluded from tsconfig.app.json so the
  test file's `node:fs` imports type-check without widening node types
  across the React app.

Install modal:
- Collapsed <details> "Already installed and the icon looks outdated?"
  with platform-tailored reinstall copy (iOS press-and-hold, macOS
  Dock drag, Android launcher uninstall, desktop fallback). Plain
  language; names the OS-cache limitation without jargon.

CLAUDE.md:
- Adds PWA_ICON_CACHE_BUST — implemented entry to Adopted Patterns.

commits.subject: refactor(pwa): harden PWA_SYSTEM — singleton, suppression, visibility, typed check
commits.body:
Align usePWAUpdate with glow-props PWA_SYSTEM pattern:

- Extract SW lifecycle state into module-level singleton (src/lib/pwa.ts)
  so registration, hasUpdate, and offlineReady survive AppLayout remounts
  instead of re-firing "update available" banners after route changes.
- Thin React wrapper uses useSyncExternalStore for tearing-safe sync.
- 30-second wasJustUpdated() suppression via sessionStorage — onNeedRefresh
  skips while the post-reload SW lifecycle settles.
- visibilitychange listener triggers registration.update() when the tab
  regains focus (catches updates faster than the 60min poll, esp. on iOS).
- controllerchange reload guard registered once; auto-reloads only when
  _userClickedUpdate is set so background tab lifecycle events don't yank
  the page out from under the user.
- checkForUpdate(): Promise<'no-sw'|'done'|'error'> for toast feedback.
- onRegisterError routes to debugLog for DebugPill surfacing.
- Manifest: id, scope, prefer_related_applications (stable identity +
  avoid Chrome skipping beforeinstallprompt).
- Workbox: cleanupOutdatedCaches + explicit globPatterns.

Preserves existing offlineReady + dismissOfflineReady in the hook return
since AppLayout uses the banner; swap to Toast system when it lands.

commits.subject: feat(scripts): generate-theme-meta.mjs — drift detection + auto-fix
commits.body:
Closes out the last deferred pattern item from THEME_DARK_MODE. Script
verifies that every combo's metaColorLight/metaColorDark hex matches the
DaisyUI oklch definition, across the three places these values live:
  1. src/constants/themes.ts (THEME_COMBOS)
  2. index.html inline bootstrap COMBOS map
  3. index.html static <meta name="theme-color"> tags (first combo wins
     the pre-JS fallback role)

Modes:
  - `node scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs`  — verify, exit 1 on drift
  - `node scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs --write`  — rewrite corrected hex

Accessed via `npm run check-theme-meta` for the common case.

Implementation notes:
  - Reads DaisyUI's per-theme oklch values from node_modules/daisyui/
    theme/object.js (CJS default export).
  - oklch → hex conversion inlined (~30 lines, matches pattern doc
    reference code). No color-conversion dependency.
  - Convention: light combos use their light theme's --color-primary
    (vibrant branded status bar); dark combos use their dark theme's
    --color-base-100 (seamless match to page background).
  - Catalog parsed from source via stable regex anchored on the
    `id: '...'` field; we own both the script and the file format, so
    acceptable. Script errors loudly if the catalog structure changes
    and the regex no longer matches.
  - Tested end-to-end: introduce drift → verify fails with diff →
    --write restores → verify passes → byte-for-byte identical to the
    pre-drift state.

Not wired into `npm run build` — keeps build fast. Intended usage:
manual after adding a combo / swapping DaisyUI theme / bumping DaisyUI
version.

commits.subject: feat(burger-menu): add external / iconClass / highlightColor item fields
commits.body:
Close the API gap vs the BURGER_MENU pattern's standard MenuItem interface.
All three fields were flagged in the post-picker audit as pattern features
we hadn't shipped.

- `external?: boolean` — marks an item as pointing outside the app. Renders
  a trailing ↗ icon after the label. ariaLabel gets "(opens in new tab)"
  appended so screen readers announce the behavior even when the label
  doesn't mention it. Use case (future): "User Guide", "Docs" items.
- `iconClass?: string` — per-icon color-class override. Defaults to
  `currentColor` via the item's state-derived text color. Use case
  (future): a warning-colored icon on an otherwise-neutral item.
- `highlightColor?: string` — alternative accent text-color class when
  `highlight: true`. Defaults to `text-primary`. Use case (future): a
  success-green "new!" highlight alongside the existing primary-tinted
  "Install app" item.

No current consumer uses any of these — they're shipped for pattern
conformance so a future reader comparing our impl against BURGER_MENU.md
sees the full MenuAction surface. Each field defaults to the existing
behavior when omitted, so every existing menu-item spec in the app
continues to work unchanged.

commits.subject: feat(theme-picker): wire combo picker into burger menu
commits.body:
Builds on the previous commit's combo storage. The burger menu now exposes
a theme picker section with one item per combo and a selected-state
checkmark on the active one. With only one combo today (Sancio Default —
corporate + business), the section is hidden via visible:false; it appears
automatically as soon as THEME_COMBOS gains a second entry.

- BurgerMenu: extend the items API from a flat MenuItem[] to a
  discriminated union MenuEntry = MenuAction | MenuSection. MenuAction
  is the existing item shape (kept as `MenuItem` alias for back-compat —
  no consumer churn). MenuSection adds non-interactive header rows
  rendered via DaisyUI's `menu-title` styling, automatically skipped by
  arrow-key navigation (the existing query selector matches buttons only).
  MenuAction also gains `selected?: boolean` which renders a trailing
  checkmark + accent highlight, with `aria-current="true"` for screen
  readers.
- useActiveCombo hook: reactive read of the active combo id. Two
  observers — MutationObserver on <html data-theme> (catches every
  applyTheme call: same-tab setCombo/toggleDark, cross-tab sync after
  theme.ts re-applies, OS-preference change) and storage event listener
  (catches the future case where two combos share a theme-per-mode and
  only the combo id changes — data-theme wouldn't mutate then). setState
  is idempotent so double-fires are harmless.
- AppLayout: wires both the dark-mode toggle (uses toggleDark) and the
  picker section (one MenuAction per THEME_COMBOS entry, calls setCombo,
  selected by useActiveCombo). Section + items use visible:THEME_COMBOS.
  length > 1 so the section is dormant until a second combo lands.
- CLAUDE.md → THEME_DARK_MODE: rewritten to document the combo schema +
  storage migration. BURGER_MENU deviation note updated to mention the
  hidden-when-single-combo picker.

Cross-tab sync verified by inspection: theme.ts storage handler mutates
data-theme; useActiveCombo's MutationObserver fires; AppLayout re-renders
the picker with the new selected state.

commits.subject: refactor(theme): migrate to combo-based storage (Approach B)
commits.body:
Storage schema changes from Chunk 7's 1-key model to pattern-doc Approach B:

  Before:  localStorage["theme"]      = "corporate" | "business"
  After:   localStorage["darkMode"]   = "true" | "false"
           localStorage["themeCombo"] = combo id

Rationale: a combo catalog is on the roadmap (user direction: "we might
add more combos later"). With combos, the active DaisyUI theme is derived
at runtime from combo + dark state, which needs both keys. The Chunk 7
single-theme model stored the resolved theme name directly, which can't
round-trip through a toggle ("I picked the Luxe combo, then switched to
light" needs to remember it was Luxe, not just the active theme name).

- src/constants/themes.ts: ThemeCombo interface + THEME_COMBOS catalog
  (1 entry today: 'default' pairing corporate/business) + helpers
  (validComboId, getCombo, resolveTheme, resolveMetaColor). Each combo
  carries metaColorLight/Dark hex for the <meta name="theme-color"> tags;
  convention follows the pattern doc — light=primary, dark=base-100.
  Adding a combo requires (1) updating the DaisyUI plugin list in
  index.css, (2) adding an entry here, (3) mirroring in index.html's
  inline bootstrap COMBOS map (documented in both places).
- src/utils/theme.ts:
    * applyTheme() signature narrowed: (dark, themeName, metaColor). Pure
      DOM mutation, no storage side-effects. Previous overloaded behavior
      split into setCombo / toggleDark / initTheme.
    * setCombo(comboId): switch combos while keeping current dark mode.
    * toggleDark(): flip dark for the active combo.
    * initTheme(): one-shot legacy migration of the old `theme` key,
      then resolve state (storage > OS preference), apply, attach
      cross-tab + OS-preference listeners. Cross-tab sync now watches
      both darkMode and themeCombo keys.
    * getActiveComboId(): live read for picker UI.
    * THEME_COMBOS re-exported for convenience.
- index.html inline bootstrap:
    * Embeds the COMBOS map inline (unavoidable — inline scripts can't
      import ES modules, documented).
    * Runs the same legacy-key migration so first paint after upgrade
      matches the migrated storage state.
    * Resolves theme from combo + dark before applying.
- AppLayout.tsx: dark-mode toggle now calls `toggleDark()` instead of
  `applyTheme()` directly. Menu-item behavior unchanged.

Legacy storage migration: one-shot. Reads `localStorage["theme"]` once,
translates to darkMode + themeCombo, removes the old key. Runs in both
the inline bootstrap (so the migrated state applies on first paint) and
theme.ts initTheme (so both code paths are defensive). Safe against
repeated runs because the old key is cleared.

No UI changes yet — picker lands in the next commit.

Accessible dropdown nav using the WAI-ARIA disclosure pattern (NOT
role="menu" — that's for application menus like File/Edit/View and causes
screen readers to enter forms mode, suppress normal nav keys, and expect
arrow-key navigation from every item).

Spec-level behavior:
- Trigger `<button aria-expanded aria-controls aria-label>` with 44×44 min
  touch target (btn-circle override for WCAG 2.5.5 + iOS HIG).
- Panel `<nav id role="navigation" aria-label>` positioned top-right of
  the trigger with max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)] so it can't overflow narrow
  viewports.
- Backdrop `<div class="fixed inset-0 cursor-pointer">` at z-40. The
  cursor-pointer is load-bearing: iOS Safari silently drops click events
  on empty divs without it.
- z-50 menu / z-40 backdrop (per Z_INDEX_SCALE). Debug pill at z-[80]
  still renders above the menu.
- Focus management via useDisclosureFocus: open → first item; close →
  back to trigger.
- Escape closes via useEscapeKey.
- ArrowDown / ArrowUp cycle items with wrap. Home / End jump to
  first / last. Tab / Shift+Tab work as browser-default.
- Close-then-act pattern: after any item action, menu closes then runs
  the action 150ms later so navigation / modal-opens happen on a clean
  DOM. Pending timer cleaned up on unmount.

MenuItem interface exposes: label, action, visible, disabled, separator,
destructive, highlight, icon (SVG path string), keepOpen (skip auto-close
for theme-toggle-style repeat-use actions), ariaLabel (screen-reader
override when label flips per state).

Action-error routing: failures in item.action surface through debugLog
('render','error',...) so they land in the DebugPill buffer rather than
vanishing into a rejected Promise.

commits.subject: feat(hooks+version): shared disclosure hooks + Vite-injected app version
commits.body:
Groundwork for the BurgerMenu component. Nothing visible yet — these are
the shared primitives the menu (and future disclosures) will consume.

- src/hooks/useEscapeKey.ts: keydown listener bound only while `active`.
  preventDefault() so the browser doesn't also eat Escape (e.g. exiting
  fullscreen) when a menu consumes it.
- src/hooks/useDisclosureFocus.ts: on open → focus first focusable in the
  content (scheduled via rAF so content has painted); on close → return
  focus to the trigger. `hasBeenOpenRef` guard prevents focus-stealing on
  initial mount.
- src/hooks/useDarkClass.ts: reactive .dark-on-<html> state via
  MutationObserver. DebugPill used to define this locally; extracted so
  BurgerMenu (main tree) and DebugPill (separate React root) share one
  implementation. Both roots read DOM directly, so no React-context
  coupling crosses the root boundary.
- src/components/common/DebugPill.tsx: drop the inline useDarkClass,
  import from the shared hook. Zero behavior change.

App version plumbing:
- vite.config.ts: read package.json once at config-load, inject as
  `__APP_VERSION__` via Vite `define`. No package metadata ships to the
  client bundle beyond the version string.
- src/vite-env.d.ts: ambient `declare const __APP_VERSION__: string`.

  is sun-sea-o) + APP_VERSION from the define (with a '0.0.0-dev'
  fallback for non-Vite contexts like tests).

commits.subject: polish(ui): audit LOW items — radius tokens, focus ring offsets, tablist ARIA
commits.body:
Close out the LOW-severity findings from the visual/a11y audit. Each item
is a small correctness or polish improvement.

- SigningStatus.tsx / SequenceSignOff.tsx: add explanatory comments for
  the intentional inline-default-rounded / full-width-rounded-none
  distinction and the `flex-col items-start` on SequenceSignOff's
  multi-line info alert. The pattern wasn't wrong — it was just undocumented.
- ModuleSummaryCard.tsx + ContractUploadPreview.tsx: swap `rounded-lg`
  for DaisyUI's `rounded-box` token. They resolve to the same pixel value
  today but `rounded-box` is the theme-controlled radius variable, so
  future theme changes (different border-radius) won't leave these two
  surfaces stuck at a fixed size.
- Modal.tsx close button: add `focus:ring-offset-2`. At 44px with
  `btn-circle`, the circular border was clipping the focus ring; the
  offset pushes the ring outside the radius so keyboard focus is visible.
- DebugPill.tsx: add `role="tablist"` + `aria-label="Debug views"` on the
  tab container, `role="tab"` + `aria-selected` on each tab button, and
  aria-labels on the Copy / Clear action buttons. The pill lives in a
  separate React root so screen readers treated it as an island of
  unlabeled buttons; now the tab pattern is announced correctly.
- Input.tsx / TextArea.tsx: add `block` to the `label-text font-medium`
  wrapper. DaisyUI's `label-text` is inline-block by default; on narrow
  (320px) viewports the label could wrap inline next to the input. `block`
  forces the label to always stack above.

Not changed (verification-blockers — require browser / device):
- The focus-ring offset on Modal close is a best-effort fix based on the
  known clipping behavior of circular focus rings. Real-device verification
  is still needed.

commits.body:
Three copies of STATUS_BADGE had drifted from each other:
- AgreementCard.tsx had draft/requested/in_progress/fully_agreed/reopened
  but lacked an `error` variant (intentional — Agreement.status has no
  error state).
- AgreementHeader.tsx omitted `draft` entirely (intentional — header
  doesn't show a badge when the agreement is untouched) but the omission
  was invisible because the map shape didn't document it.
- ProposalCard.tsx had its own Proposal.status map (pending/accepted/
  rejected/countered) inline.

New src/constants/statusBadge.ts exports AGREEMENT_STATUS_BADGE and
PROPOSAL_STATUS_BADGE, typed as Record<AgreementStatus, BadgeSpec> and
Record<ProposalStatus, BadgeSpec>. The exhaustive Record type now catches
any new enum value at compile time.

- AgreementCard: imports AGREEMENT_STATUS_BADGE; `?? fallback` syntax
  replaces `|| fallback` (nullish-coalescing is correct here — BadgeSpec
  objects are always truthy).
- AgreementHeader: imports AGREEMENT_STATUS_BADGE; explicit
  `status === 'draft' ? null` branch now documents why draft renders
  without a badge.
- ProposalCard: imports PROPOSAL_STATUS_BADGE.

Two separate maps rather than a single generic one because Agreement and
Proposal are genuinely different enums with different semantic meanings
(agreement lifecycle vs proposal resolution). Keeping them co-located in
one file makes future drift easy to spot.

commits.subject: fix(a11y): restore WCAG 2.5.5 touch targets + theme-aware skip link
commits.body:
The DaisyUI migration inadvertently regressed several mobile touch targets
and broke the keyboard skip-to-content link's theme adaptation. All caught
by the post-migration a11y/mobile/dark-mode audit.

- src/index.css: .skip-to-content reads `var(--color-primary)` +
  `var(--color-primary-content)` instead of hardcoded #1e293b / #fff. DaisyUI
  exposes these per theme, so the focused skip pill now has high contrast
  against both corporate and business page surfaces (it was near-invisible
  on business's #202020 page bg before).
- NotificationBell.tsx: "Mark all read" btn-xs (24-28px) → btn-sm +
  min-h-[44px]. Delete icon btn-sm btn-square (32px) gets min-h-[44px]
  min-w-[44px]. Both failed WCAG 2.5.5.
- PwaInstallPrompt.tsx: Install / How-to-Install CTAs swap from naked
  `btn btn-sm` (32px) to `<Button size="sm">` (40px with the wrapper's
  WCAG override). Dismiss btn-sm btn-circle (32px) gets min-h-[44px]
  min-w-[44px].
- Input.tsx: every form input gets `min-h-[44px] text-base`. DaisyUI's
  input-bordered defaults to 40px / 14px — iOS Safari auto-zooms on focus
  when font-size < 16px. text-base (16px) + 44px height fixes both.
- TextArea.tsx: text-base added (no height override — textareas are
  multi-row by nature).
- ProposeChangeForm.tsx + ModuleEditor.tsx content textareas + AddNoteForm
  note textarea: text-base added on the direct DaisyUI `textarea
  textarea-bordered` usage (these bypass the TextArea wrapper).
- AddNoteForm.tsx source select: min-h-[44px] text-base on
  `select select-bordered`.

Net: every primary form input on mobile now prevents iOS auto-zoom + meets
WCAG 2.5.5; every icon button in the migrated code path now hits 44×44.

commits.subject: feat(debug-pill): theme-aware palette + document exceptions
commits.body:
DebugPill lives in a separate React root so it can't consume theme context
or DaisyUI classes. Per the DEBUG_SYSTEM pattern, swap palettes by reading
the `.dark` class from `<html>` directly via a MutationObserver.

- LIGHT / DARK Palette constants: panel bg/border/header, muted tiers,
  tab-active surface, severity bg/fg (error/warn/success/info), diagnostic
  status colors (pass/fail/warn/running), and per-source accent colors.
  Dark variants use slate-900/800 surfaces with slate-300/400 text and
  deep-tinted severity backgrounds (#450a0a for error etc.) so contrast
  stays above WCAG AA.
- `createStyles(palette)` factory replaces the static `S` Record;
  tabStyle/logRowStyle/logSourceStyle/diagStatusStyle take the palette
  as an extra arg.
- `useDarkClass()` hook: subscribes to <html>'s class attribute mutations.
  applyTheme() changes `.dark` in a single mutation, so no storm.
- Pill chip itself stays dark in both themes — it's a floating diagnostic
  that needs to be visible against any page surface; themeing it to
  base-100 would make it invisible on corporate pages.
- Adds `theme` to the recognized sources map (the Chunk 7 debugLog emits
  use it) with a DaisyUI-primary-ish purple accent.

Patterns → THEME_DARK_MODE. Lists every file that legitimately keeps
hardcoded hex/colors (tones palette, theme source of truth, inline
bootstrap, print overrides, debug pill) with the reason each is preserved,
plus a grep command for catching regressions.

  theme palette.

(DaisyUI's rhythm differs from the prior custom), themed focus rings, and
darker elevated surfaces in corporate mode (base-200 vs old slate-50).

commits.subject: fix(hooks): eliminate set-state-in-effect cascades in useModules + DebugPill
commits.body:
Two pre-existing react-hooks/set-state-in-effect errors, both flagged by
react-hooks v7 under React 19. Each is a real cascading-render pattern
that would cause redundant renders on every dependency change.

useModules.ts: a separate useEffect watched `modules` and called
fetchVersions() synchronously, which setState'd versions. Restructure so
fetchVersions takes an explicit module list and is called inline at the
end of fetchModules (with the just-fetched data) and from the realtime
INSERT subscription via `modulesRef.current`. The reactive-effect pattern
is gone; version fetching now chains directly off each module load.

Side benefit: `fetchVersions` no longer depends on `modules` closure, so
its identity is stable across module changes — the subscription effect

commits.subject: fix(pre-react-chrome): align boot loader + overlays + manifest with theme
commits.body:
Pre-React chrome (boot-loader, noscript fallback, fatal-error overlay in
index.html, App-load-failure fallback in main.tsx) used hardcoded slate
hex values — a hard light-mode flash before React mounts under business.
Fix by having the inline bootstrap script set CSS custom properties on
<html> before first paint; every pre-React surface now reads var(--boot-*)
with a hex fallback.

- index.html inline script: sets --boot-bg, --boot-fg, --boot-muted,
  --boot-spinner, --boot-error-bg, --boot-error-fg based on resolved
  theme (stored preference → OS preference).
- index.html boot-loader, noscript, error overlay: colors read var(--boot-*).
- main.tsx App-load fallback: same treatment.
- src/utils/theme.ts: new updateBootChromeColors(dark) called from
  applyTheme so user-explicit theme changes also refresh the palette
  (e.g. if React fails after a runtime theme toggle, overlay matches).
- vite.config.ts PWA manifest theme_color: #1e293b → #202020 (business
  base-100). Static once installed; dark is forgiving under both app
  themes where a branded primary would clash on dark-mode devices.

Inline script's palette mirrors updateBootChromeColors() in theme.ts —
unavoidable duplication per the pattern (inline scripts can't import
ES modules). Both sites noted so they stay in sync.

Also adds a CSP caveat comment: strict `script-src` without 'unsafe-inline'
will block the bootstrap; precompute a SHA-256 hash if Sancio adopts one.

commits.subject: refactor(debug): harden DEBUG_SYSTEM — 10-part refactor per pattern
commits.body:
Applies the glow-props DEBUG_SYSTEM pattern end-to-end:

src/utils/debugLog.ts (rewritten):
- A) DebugEntry gains numeric `id` (auto-incrementing) for stable React
     keys + dedup.
- B) DebugEntry gains optional `details: Record<string, unknown>` for
     structured post-mortem payloads. debugLog() takes an optional 4th arg.
- C) DebugSource is now a union with `(string & {})` fallback — known
     sources keep autocomplete, ad-hoc strings work without enum edits.
- F) `debugGenerateReport()` moved out of DebugPill into the module;
     reusable by any consumer.
- G) URL query strings redacted in reports — both the env URL line and
     any URL-ish substring inside entry events / stringified details.
- H) Global listeners (console.error/warn interception, window error +
     unhandledrejection) are now installed exactly once via the
     `window.__debugLogListenersAttached` flag, preventing HMR duplicates.
     The old `window.onerror = …` + `addEventListener('error', …)` double
     wiring is gone.
- I) `subscribe()` now replays existing entries to each new subscriber
     so late-mounting consumers see the full boot sequence.
- J) Every subscriber invocation is wrapped in try/catch — one throwing
     listener can't break the rest. `clearEntries()` now sends a 'clear'
     signal via the same notify path (cleaner than the previous
     `message === '__clear__'` sentinel).

The internal `message` field is renamed to `event` to match the pattern.
All external callers pass strings positionally, so no call-site changes
were needed outside DebugPill.

src/components/common/DebugPill.tsx (rewritten):
- D) Every Tailwind class replaced with inline `CSSProperties` style
     objects. The pill renders in an isolated React root (#debug-root),
     so a Tailwind build failure or delayed CSS load no longer affects
     its rendering.
- E) New "PWA" tab alongside Log/Env. Runs live diagnostics on open:
     HTTPS, network, SW support + registration state, manifest fetch +
     validation, standalone mode, beforeinstallprompt receipt. A
     monotonic run counter drops stale results if the tab is closed
     mid-probe.
- Entries are keyed by the new numeric `id` field.
- `Copy` now calls `debugGenerateReport()` from the module.
- `details` payload displayed inline on log entries.

src/types/browser.d.ts:
- Window gains optional `__debugLogListenersAttached` flag so the HMR
  guard is type-safe.

Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: cross-fleet-docs
Semver: patch
commits.title: harden DEBUG_SYSTEM — 10-part refactor per pattern

commits.subject: fix(modules): resolve 10 issues in contract upload feature
commits.body:
Bugs fixed:
- Stale props: ContractUploadPreview ignored new candidates when
  sub-section toggle changed. Added key prop to force remount.
- Duplicate flow infinite loop: "Upload anyway" re-opened file picker
  which re-triggered duplicate detection. Now stores ParseResult in
  duplicate state and proceeds directly to preview.
- Retry progress tracking: retrying failed modules replaced the entire
  progress array with a shorter one, losing successful items. Now merges
  retry results back at the correct indices.
- hardSplitAtWordBoundary title count: used pre-filter chunk count in
  titles like "(2/5)" when only 3 chunks existed. Now filters first.
- handleSplitOversized double computation: called splitOversizedSection
  twice. Now computes once and reuses.

Standards fixes:
- Touch targets: preview move buttons were 36px, below WCAG 2.5.5
  minimum 44px. Matched to existing ModuleCard pattern.
- Duplicated MODULE_CONTENT_LIMIT: exported from contractSplitter,
  imported in ContractUploadPreview instead of redeclaring.
- BATCH_SIZE inside component: moved to module scope as UPLOAD_BATCH_SIZE.
- PDF type annotation: replaced complex conditional type inference with
  simple let + await pattern, letting TypeScript infer the type.
- recordUpload on partial failure: hash was recorded even when some
  modules failed, causing false duplicate warnings on retry. Now only
  records on full success.

commits.subject: feat(modules): add contract upload and conversion to modules
commits.body:
Upload traditional contracts (.txt, .docx, .pdf) and convert them into
Sancio's module-based structure. Client-side extraction with no backend
changes needed.

Key design decisions:
- PDF library (pdfjs-dist) lazy-loaded via dynamic import with Vite-bundled
  worker — zero cost for non-PDF users, works offline (no CDN dependency)
- Heuristic section splitting at depth 0 only by default to avoid module
  proliferation; sub-section splitting is opt-in
- Batch creation in parallel groups of 4 via Promise.allSettled to handle
  SA mobile latency on 30+ module uploads
- Source tracking via [Source: filename] note convention — syncs to both
  parties via Supabase, searchable, no schema changes needed
- Duplicate detection via text hash in localStorage
- Encoding detection: BOM → UTF-8 strict → Windows-1252 fallback
- Oversized sections (>10k chars) split via paragraph → sentence →
  word-boundary fallback chain

New files:
- src/utils/documentParser.ts — text extraction for .txt/.docx/.pdf
- src/utils/contractSplitter.ts — heuristic section splitting
- src/components/modules/ContractUploadPreview.tsx — preview/edit UI

Modified files:
- src/components/modules/TemplateSelector.tsx — tabbed UI with upload tab
- src/pages/AgreementDetailPage.tsx — batch creation handler

commits.subject: fix(boot): decouple DebugPill from App and add zero-dependency error overlay
commits.body:
The blank white screen with no debug pill happens because main.tsx
statically imports both App and DebugPill. If any module in App's deep
dependency tree (react-router, supabase, pages, hooks) throws during
evaluation, the entire ES module fails and DebugPill never mounts —
leaving the user with zero diagnostic information.

Three changes to make the boot process resilient:

1. Dynamic import for App — DebugPill now mounts first via static import
   (tiny chain: React + debugLog). App loads asynchronously via import().
   If App's chunk fails, DebugPill still renders and shows the error.

2. Inline error overlay in index.html — a classic (non-module) script
   that catches errors and shows them on screen before any React code
   runs. Also includes a 10-second boot timeout to catch silent failures.

3. Boot-stage logging — debugLog calls at each boot stage (main.tsx
   executing, DebugPill mounted, App loading, App rendered, Supabase
   config status) so the debug pill shows exactly where things went wrong.

Tags: boot, debug, resilience, blank-screen

commits.subject: fix(boot): add 404.html fallback and inline loading indicator
commits.body:
GitHub Pages returns its default 404 for any path that doesn't map to a
real file. After React Router navigates to /login or /projects (client-
side), a hard refresh (pulldown on mobile) asks GitHub Pages for that
path — which doesn't exist. Without a 404.html the response is empty,
producing the blank screen the user sees (no HTML, no JS, no DebugPill).

Changes:
- deploy.yml: copy index.html → 404.html after build so GitHub Pages
  serves the SPA shell for every route
- index.html: add an inline loading spinner inside #root that is visible
  immediately while JS downloads; React's createRoot().render() replaces
  #root's children, removing it automatically. Also add <noscript>
  fallback for disabled-JS browsers.
- usePWAUpdate.ts: prefix unused swUrl param to fix TS6133

commits.body:
Complete Phase 1 (Foundation) frontend for Sancio agreement builder.
Frontend-only with mock data and localStorage persistence, designed
for clean swap to Supabase backend when ready.

- Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4 + PWA scaffold
- Shared type definitions (Project, Agreement, Module, User)
- Reusable component library (Button, Input, TextArea, Modal, EmptyState, etc.)
- Mock auth with AuthContext + localStorage
- CRUD hooks (useProjects, useAgreements, useModules) with localStorage
- 6 starter module templates
- All pages: Login, ProjectList, ProjectDetail, AgreementDetail, ModuleEditor, 404
- React Router v7 with protected routes and breadcrumbs
- Module reorder (up/down), inline editing, delete confirmations
- Product definition saved as docs/PRODUCT_DEFINITION.md
- Backend spec started as docs/BACKEND_SPEC.md
